System instructions:

You are a focused Lean 4 formalization worker operating inside a local Mathlib project. You may edit files and run local commands within this one Codex run. Work on exactly one scratch file, keep the formalization local and faithful to the target node, prefer the most concrete faithful theorem you can manage, and stop when the scratch file is in its best current state. Return only JSON matching the supplied schema.

User task:

Theorem title: Weak formulation of the Poisson problem via Lax–Milgram

Ambient theorem statement:
Weak formulation of the Poisson problem via Lax–Milgram.

Let \(\Omega \subset \mathbb{R}^d\) be a bounded domain with sufficiently regular boundary, and let \(f \in L^2(\Omega)\). Then there exists a unique \(u \in H_0^1(\Omega)\) such that
\[
\int_\Omega \nabla u \cdot \nabla v\,dx
=
\int_\Omega f v\,dx
\qquad \text{for all } v \in H_0^1(\Omega).
\]
Moreover,
\[
\|u\|_{H_0^1(\Omega)} \le C \|f\|_{L^2(\Omega)}.
\]

Target node:

{
  "id": "n1",
  "title": "Boundedness of the Dirichlet bilinear form",
  "informal_statement": "For all \\(u,v\\in H_0^1(\\Omega)\\),\n\\[\n|a(u,v)|=\\left|\\int_\\Omega \\nabla u\\cdot\\nabla v\\,dx\\right|\n\\le \\|u\\|_{H_0^1(\\Omega)}\\,\\|v\\|_{H_0^1(\\Omega)}.\n\\]\nHence \\(a\\) is bounded on \\(H_0^1(\\Omega)\\times H_0^1(\\Omega)\\).",
  "informal_proof_text": "Apply Cauchy\u2013Schwarz to the integral of \\(\\nabla u\\cdot\\nabla v\\):\n\\[\n\\left|\\int_\\Omega \\nabla u\\cdot\\nabla v\\,dx\\right|\n\\le \\left(\\int_\\Omega |\\nabla u|^2\\,dx\\right)^{1/2}\n\\left(\\int_\\Omega |\\nabla v|^2\\,dx\\right)^{1/2}.\n\\]\nOn \\(H_0^1(\\Omega)\\), this is exactly the \\(H_0^1\\)-norm bound used here.",
  "formalization_priority": 3,
  "formalization_rationale": "Clean local inequality for the Dirichlet form; straightforward but still useful as a reusable boundedness fact for the application node."
}

Preserve the ambient mathematical setting of the theorem and node. If the node is stated in a concrete setting, keep that same setting in the Lean theorem unless the node itself explicitly states a more abstract generality.

Current scratch file to revise:

```lean
import Mathlib.MeasureTheory.Function.L2Space
import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.PiL2

open MeasureTheory

local notation "⟪" x ", " y "⟫" => inner ℝ x y

theorem dirichlet_form_bounded
    {α : Type*} [MeasurableSpace α] {μ : Measure α} {n : ℕ}
    (grad_u grad_v : MeasureTheory.Lp (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin n)) 2 μ) :
    |∫ x, ⟪grad_u x, grad_v x⟫ ∂μ| ≤ ‖grad_u‖ * ‖grad_v‖ := by
  simpa [MeasureTheory.L2.inner_def] using abs_real_inner_le_norm grad_u grad_v

```

import Mathlib

noncomputable section

open scoped ENNReal

abbrev L2Space (d : ℕ) :=
  MeasureTheory.Lp ℂ (2 : ℝ≥0∞) (volume : Measure (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin d)))

theorem nls_mass_conservation_local_identity
    {d : ℕ}
    (u : ℝ → L2Space d)
    (u0 : L2Space d)
    (h_init : u 0 = u0)
    (h_mass : ∀ t : ℝ, ‖u t‖ = ‖u 0‖)
    (h_nontrivial : 0 < ‖u0‖) :
    ∃ M : ℝ, 0 < M ∧ ∀ t : ℝ, ‖u t‖ = M := by
  refine ⟨‖u0‖, h_nontrivial, ?_⟩
  intro t
  rw [h_mass t, h_init]

exec

import Mathlib.MeasureTheory.Integral.Bochner.Basic
import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.Basic
import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.PiL2
import Mathlib.MeasureTheory.Measure.Haar.InnerProductSpace

open MeasureTheory

namespace FormalIslands

theorem n_energy_split
    (d : ℕ)
    (Ω : Set (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin d)))
    (grad_u grad_w : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin d) → EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin d))
    (hu : Integrable (fun x => ‖grad_u x‖ ^ 2) (volume.restrict Ω))
    (hw : Integrable (fun x => ‖grad_w x‖ ^ 2) (volume.restrict Ω))
    (huw : Integrable (fun x => @inner ℝ _ _ (grad_u x) (grad_w x)) (volume.restrict Ω))
    (horth : ∫ x, @inner ℝ _ _ (grad_u x) (grad_w x) ∂(volume.restrict Ω) = 0) :
    (∫ x, ‖grad_u x + grad_w x‖ ^ 2 ∂(volume.restrict Ω)) =
      (∫ x, ‖grad_u x‖ ^ 2 ∂(volume.restrict Ω)) +
        ∫ x, ‖grad_w x‖ ^ 2 ∂(volume.restrict Ω) := by
  have h_expand :
      (fun x => ‖grad_u x + grad_w x‖ ^ 2) =
        fun x => ‖grad_u x‖ ^ 2 + 2 * @inner ℝ _ _ (grad_u x) (grad_w x) + ‖grad_w x‖ ^ 2 := by
    funext x
    simpa using norm_add_sq_real (grad_u x) (grad_w x)
  rw [h_expand]
  have h_two_inner :
      Integrable (fun x => 2 * @inner ℝ _ _ (grad_u x) (grad_w x)) (volume.restrict Ω) :=
    huw.const_mul (2 : ℝ)
  have h_first_two :
      Integrable (fun x => ‖grad_u x‖ ^ 2 + 2 * @inner ℝ _ _ (grad_u x) (grad_w x))
        (volume.restrict Ω) :=
    hu.add h_two_inner
  have h_assoc :
      (fun x => ‖grad_u x‖ ^ 2 + 2 * @inner ℝ _ _ (grad_u x) (grad_w x) + ‖grad_w x‖ ^ 2) =
        fun x => (‖grad_u x‖ ^ 2 + 2 * @inner ℝ _ _ (grad_u x) (grad_w x)) + ‖grad_w x‖ ^ 2 := by
    funext x
    ring
  rw [h_assoc, integral_add h_first_two hw, integral_add hu h_two_inner,
    integral_const_mul, horth]
  ring

end FormalIslands
